key_insight: A D-gun applied Al2O3-TiO2 coating substantially improves the wear resistance of Ti-6Al-4V, and random forest predicts wear most accurately from sliding conditions.
engineering_relevance: The study links detonation spray coating process parameters with tribological performance, offering a practical route to extend the service life of aerospace, biomedical, and marine Ti-6Al-4V components.
method: Applied ~200 µm Al2O3-TiO2 ceramic coating on Ti-6Al-4V using detonation gun spraying.
Measured wear via ASTM G99 pin-on-disc tests against EN31 steel at 40–100 N, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min.
Built ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from test conditions.
evidence_signal: SEM showed reduced wear and a protective tribo-layer on coated surfaces; random forest gave the lowest mean percentage error and mean squared error.
watch_item: The results are based on a limited experimental window and specific coating/process settings, so the model and coating performance may not generalize without further validation.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
